footer.tagline: Setting standards in postpartum care through culturally integrated training, certification, and multilingual support for Asian doulas.
footer.newsletterDescription: Training updates, certification news, and community stories.
footer.rights: Asian Doula Alliance. All rights reserved.

home.whoWeAreTitle: Leading in Professional Asian Postpartum Doula Care Since 2017!
home.whoWeAreText: Empowering families with specialized and reliable Asian Style Postpartum Care, we are the only U.S. doula organization offering multilingual support and certified training by uniquely blend traditional Asian confinement practices with modern, professional postpartum and newborn care.
home.howToGetCertifiedSubtitle: Guiding you through every step with care, clarity, and cultural understanding.
home.step1Desc: Visit our Enrollment Page to provide your name, email, and a brief description of your experience and interests. After submitting your application, an ADA specialist will contact you to discuss the program details and guide you through the next steps.
home.step2Desc: Engage in training that integrates traditional Asian postpartum practices with modern, evidence-based care. Choose from in-person workshops or online modules to suit your schedule and learning preferences.
home.step3Desc: The certification exam includes both a written assessment and a practical evaluation to test your knowledge and skills. Utilize ADA's study guides and practice materials to prepare effectively for the exam.
home.step4Desc: Upon passing the exam, you will be awarded the ADA Postpartum Doula Certification. Benefit from ADA's continuous education opportunities, professional resources, and a supportive community to help you thrive in your doula career.
home.ourValuesSubtitle: Compassion, professionalism, dedication, and cultural understanding define our care.
home.valueTrustDesc: Providing honest and empathetic care to every family, fostering a foundation of respect and reliability.
home.valueExcellenceDesc: Committed to the highest standards through comprehensive training and culturally attuned support.
home.valueCommitmentDesc: Supporting mothers' recovery and newborn care with unwavering dedication at every stage.
home.valueCulturalDesc: Harmonizing traditional Asian practices with modern care to serve families globally.
home.ourWorkSubtitle: Elevating postpartum care with culturally rooted and evidence-based programs.
home.doulaCertificationDesc: We provide structured training and certification with a strong emphasis on Asian cultural practices, science-based knowledge, and hands-on support.
home.scholarshipProgramDesc: We offer need-based and merit-based scholarships to make doula training accessible for passionate caregivers in Asian communities.
home.familyMatchingDesc: We carefully match families with trained doulas who understand both your cultural background and modern recovery needs.
home.multilingualTrainingDesc: We offer education and care services in Mandarin, Cantonese, Korean, Japanese, and more — because care starts with clear understanding.
home.culturalPostpartumCareDesc: From confinement meals to herbal remedies and body recovery practices, we guide doulas and families in integrating trusted Asian traditions.
home.communityAdvocacyDesc: We champion postpartum care rights, organize workshops, and empower Asian doulas and families to be seen, heard, and supported.
home.ourPartnersSubtitle: Trusted collaborations with leading organizations in maternal care.
home.iceaDesc: A globally respected organization offering evidence-based training for childbirth educators and doulas. ADA collaborates with ICEA to promote excellence in maternal and infant health worldwide.
home.donaDesc: As one of the largest and most established doula organizations, DONA International partners with ADA to ensure high standards of training, certification, and professional support for doulas.
home.tebDesc: ADA proudly partners with The Educated Birth to promote birthwork that is informed, equitable, and rooted in justice. Together, we support doulas of all backgrounds with inclusive tools and resources.
home.cooingsDesc: A partner in innovation, Cooings provides professional postpartum services while pioneering modern doula training for Asian caregivers through digital tools and data-driven solutions.
home.iblceDesc: ADA aligns with IBLCE in promoting breastfeeding education and certification. We support doulas who wish to expand their knowledge in lactation support and pursue IBCLC credentials.
home.verifyCtaDesc: Insurance providers, healthcare facilities, and families can instantly verify any ADA-credentialed doula by name, Doula ID, or certificate number.
home.latestArticlesSubtitle: News, insights, and resources from ADA.
home.beginDoulaJourneyDesc: Our ADA-approved training programs combine traditional Asian postpartum practices with modern evidence-based care. Programs available in English, Chinese, Japanese, and Korean.
home.whereDoulasServeDesc: 167+ certified doulas supporting families across California and nationwide.
home.alsoServingCities: Also serving families in Chicago, Houston, and other cities nationwide

aboutUs.heroDescription: The Asian Doula Alliance (ADA) (亚洲月嫂联盟) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization dedicated to supporting and advancing the education of Asian doulas.
aboutUs.whyADADescription: The Asian postpartum community has faced troubling challenges, including severe breaches of trust such as “The Chinese Doula Incident In Bay Area”, where a doula was caught on camera allegedly abusing a 2-day-old infant, underscores the critical need for rigorous standards and professional oversight in postpartum care. These events have left families vulnerable and distrustful.
aboutUs.howADAHelpDescription: ADA was founded to respond to such urgent needs by introducing structured training, stringent certification, and a robust ethical framework. Our certification process involves rigorous screening, continuous education, and clear accountability standards, ensuring doulas uphold the highest professional and ethical conduct.
aboutUs.ourValuesDescription: Compassion, professionalism, dedication, and cultural understanding define our care.

aboutHistory.heroDescription: From a WeChat group to the only Asian doula certification body in the U.S.
aboutHistory.historyDescription: From a WeChat group to the only Asian doula certification body in the U.S.
aboutHistory.founderQuote: It started as a chat group. It became a mission.
aboutHistory.missionOfficial: In 2022, that mission became official. ADA was incorporated as a 501(c)(3) non-profit in Irvine, California — the first and only U.S. organization offering culturally integrated postpartum doula certification with exams in five languages.
aboutHistory.trainingScenes: Scenes from ADA training workshops in Irvine, California
aboutHistory.milestonesDescription: From 0 to 167+ certified doulas in five years.
aboutHistory.ctaDescription: Join 167+ certified doulas making a difference in families' lives.

aboutMission.aboutHeroDescription: Setting the standard for culturally integrated postpartum care
aboutMission.aboutMissionDescription: Empowering Asian families, we are the only U.S. doula organization blending traditional practices with modern care, offering multilingual support and certified training for a culturally rich postpartum experience.
aboutMission.aboutBlockquote: ADA was founded to respond to urgent community needs by introducing structured training, stringent certification, and a robust ethical framework. Through these efforts, we rebuild and fortify trust, ensuring every family receives reliable, safe, and culturally respectful postpartum support.
aboutMission.aboutQuoteCard1: Our rigorous certification and ongoing training ensure doulas uphold the highest ethics, responsibility, and care for Asian families. We are committed to restoring trust through strict professional standards.
aboutMission.aboutQuoteCard1Author: Director of Education, ADA Certification & Training
aboutMission.aboutVisionDescription: Our vision is to be the leading authority in postpartum care, setting the standard for culturally integrated support that honors Asian traditions while embracing modern care practices, ensuring every family receives compassionate, comprehensive, and culturally resonant support.
aboutMission.aboutQuoteCard2: We strive to rebuild and sustain community trust through meticulous training, clear ethical guidelines, and strong support networks, ensuring that doulas are held accountable and families receive reliable, safe, and culturally respectful postpartum support.
aboutMission.aboutCommitmentDescription: The standards we hold ourselves to, every single day.

aboutBoard.heroDescription: The volunteers who guide ADA's mission and hold us accountable.
aboutBoard.electionBannerDescription: Nominations are now open for the 2026–2028 term.
aboutBoard.ourBoardDescription: ADA is governed by a volunteer Board of Directors responsible for setting strategic direction, ensuring financial accountability, and upholding the organization's mission. Board members serve two-year terms and are elected by ADA's certified doula community.
aboutBoard.bottomNote: Board members serve on a volunteer basis and receive no compensation. ADA's Form 990 is available upon request.

aboutBoardElection.introText: Every two years, ADA's certified doula community elects the Board of Directors who will guide the organization's strategic direction. The 2026 election covers all five board positions for the 2026–2028 term.
aboutBoardElection.canINominateAnswer: Yes. Any certified doula can nominate a colleague, but the nominee must confirm their willingness to serve.
aboutBoardElection.moreCandidatesThanPositions: What if more people run than positions available?
aboutBoardElection.moreCandidatesThanPositionsAnswer: If a position has multiple candidates, certified members vote by electronic ballot during the voting period.
aboutBoardElection.areBoardMembersPaidAnswer: No. All board positions are volunteer roles. Board members receive no compensation.
aboutBoardElection.termLengthAnswer: Board members serve two-year terms. There are no term limits — members may be re-elected.

certifications.heroDescription: Three certification tracks to advance your career in maternal and newborn care.
certifications.trackPostpartumDoulaDescription: The gold standard in culturally integrated postpartum care certification. Exams in 5 languages, recognized by 6 insurance partners.
certifications.trackBirthDoulaDescription: Support families through labor and delivery with culturally integrated training and ADA certification.
certifications.trackIbclcExamPrepDescription: Multilingual preparation course for the International Board Certified Lactation Consultant exam.

certPostpartum.heroDescription: The gold standard in culturally integrated postpartum care — recognized by 6 major insurance providers.
certPostpartum.introDescription: ADA's Postpartum Doula Certification is the only culturally integrated doula credential in the United States. Our certified doulas blend traditional Asian postpartum practices — including zuo yuezi (sitting the month) — with modern evidence-based care.
certPostpartum.insuranceRecognitionDescription: ADA certification is recognized by major insurance providers, making your services accessible to more families:
certPostpartum.step1: Enroll in an ADA-approved training program
certPostpartum.ctaDescription: Find an ADA-approved training program near you.

certSteps.threeStepsDescription: Three steps to becoming an ADA-certified postpartum doula.
certSteps.certificationCommitment: The ADA certification demonstrates your commitment to providing culturally sensitive, evidence-based postpartum care. Here's how to earn your credential.
certSteps.findTrainingProgramDescription: Find an ADA-approved training program and begin your journey.
certSteps.officialCertification: Official ADA certification certificate with unique exam ID
certSteps.listingInRegistry: Listing in the ADA Certified Doula Registry (publicly verifiable)
certSteps.insuranceRecognition: Recognition by major insurance providers (Medi-Cal, Kaiser, Cigna, IEHP, Carrot Fertility, Progyny)
certSteps.membershipInCommunity: Membership in the ADA professional community
certSteps.useAdaCertified: Right to use the "ADA Certified" designation
certSteps.howLongCertificationProcess: How long does the certification process take?
certSteps.certificationProcessDuration: From enrollment to certification, most doulas complete the process in 2-4 weeks. Training programs typically run 4-5 days of intensive instruction, followed by the certification exam.
certSteps.noPriorExperience: No prior doula experience is required. ADA-approved training programs are designed to take you from beginner to exam-ready. However, a genuine passion for supporting new families is essential.
certSteps.languageSupport: What if I don't speak English?
certSteps.languageTrainingSupport: ADA offers training and exams in English, Chinese (Mandarin), Japanese, and Korean. You can complete the entire certification process in your preferred language.
certSteps.totalCost: How much does it cost in total?
certSteps.examFeeDetails: The exam fee is $625. Training program fees vary by provider — contact your chosen program for pricing. Some scholarship funding may be available.
certSteps.postPassDetails: You'll receive your ADA certification (valid for 1 year), a unique exam ID, and immediate listing in our verified doula registry. Insurance providers can verify your certification through our system.
certSteps.step1Title: Enroll in an ADA-Approved Training Program
certSteps.step1Description: Find and enroll in an ADA-approved postpartum doula training program. Our approved programs cover essential skills including newborn care, postpartum recovery support, breastfeeding basics, cultural competency, and family dynamics. Training programs are available in multiple languages including English, Chinese, Japanese, and Korean.
certSteps.step2Description: Successfully complete all required coursework and hands-on training hours. Training typically includes classroom instruction, practical demonstrations, and supervised practice sessions. You will learn evidence-based techniques for supporting new families during the critical postpartum period.
certSteps.step3Description: Register for and pass the ADA Certification Examination. The exam consists of a 60-minute written test and a 30-minute practical evaluation conducted one-on-one with an examiner. The exam fee is $625 and is available in English, Chinese, Japanese, and Korean. Upon passing, you will receive your ADA certification, valid for 1 year.
certSteps.faq1Q: How long does the certification process take?
certSteps.faq1A: From enrollment to certification, most doulas complete the process in 2-4 weeks. Training programs typically run 4-5 days of intensive instruction, followed by the certification exam.
certSteps.faq2A: No prior doula experience is required. ADA-approved training programs are designed to take you from beginner to exam-ready. However, a genuine passion for supporting new families is essential.
certSteps.faq3Q: What if I don't speak English?
certSteps.faq3A: ADA offers training and exams in English, Chinese (Mandarin), Japanese, and Korean. You can complete the entire certification process in your preferred language.
certSteps.faq4Q: How much does it cost in total?
certSteps.faq4A: The exam fee is $625. Training program fees vary by provider. Some scholarship funding may be available.
certSteps.faq5A: You'll receive your ADA certification (valid for 1 year), a unique exam ID, and immediate listing in our verified doula registry. Insurance providers can verify your certification through our system.

certTraining.heroDescription: ADA-approved programs that prepare you for certification.
certTraining.becomeProviderCta: Interested in Becoming an Approved Training Provider?
certTraining.becomeProviderDescription: If you operate a doula training program and would like to apply for ADA approval, please contact us for more information on our approval process.

certExam.examOverviewDescription: The ADA Certification Examination evaluates your readiness to provide professional postpartum doula care. The exam consists of a written portion and a practical evaluation, both designed to assess your knowledge of evidence-based practices and hands-on competency. Candidates who pass receive an ADA certification valid for one year.
certExam.readyToTakeExam: Ready to take the exam? Start with Step 1.
certExam.examFormat: Multiple choice and short answer questions
certExam.examContent: Covers postpartum care, newborn care, and cultural competency
certExam.practicalExamAssessor: Assessed by a certified ADA examiner
certExam.faqWrittenExam: What does the written exam cover?
certExam.faqWrittenExamAnswer: The written exam covers postpartum care fundamentals, newborn care, breastfeeding support, cultural competency, safety protocols, and client communication. Questions are a mix of multiple choice and short answer, designed to assess your knowledge of evidence-based postpartum care practices.
certExam.faqPracticalExam: What happens during the practical exam?
certExam.faqPracticalExamAnswer: The practical exam is a 30-minute one-on-one evaluation with a certified examiner. You will demonstrate hands-on skills including newborn handling, swaddling, bathing techniques, postpartum recovery support, and your ability to communicate effectively with families from diverse cultural backgrounds.
certExam.faqNativeLanguage: Can I take the exam in my native language?
certExam.faqNativeLanguageAnswer: Yes. ADA offers the certification exam in English, Chinese (Mandarin), Japanese, and Korean. Please specify your preferred language when registering for the exam. All exam materials, including instructions and questions, will be provided in your chosen language.
certExam.faqFailExam: What if I fail the exam?
certExam.faqFailExamAnswer: If you do not pass on your first attempt, you may retake the exam for a fee of $325. You must wait at least 30 days before retaking the exam. We recommend reviewing the areas identified for improvement and consulting with your training program before reattempting.
certExam.faqRegisterExam: How do I register for the exam?
certExam.faqRegisterExamAnswer: After completing an ADA-approved training program, your training provider will guide you through the exam registration process. You can also contact ADA directly at contact@asiandoula.org or call (714) 202-6501 to register.
certExam.faqExamLocationAnswer: Exams are administered at the ADA training center located at 7515 Irvine Center Drive, #110, Irvine, CA 92618. Additional exam locations may be available depending on demand. Contact us for the latest schedule.
certExam.faqCertificationTime: How long does it take to receive my certification?
certExam.faqCertificationTimeAnswer: Results are typically communicated within 2 weeks of your exam date. Once you pass, your ADA certification and exam ID will be issued and you can begin practicing as a certified doula.

certBirth.heroSubtitle: Supporting families through labor and delivery — coming soon to ADA.
certBirth.birthDoulaDescription: A birth doula provides continuous physical, emotional, and informational support to families during labor and delivery. Unlike postpartum doulas who focus on recovery after birth, birth doulas are present throughout the birthing process — from early labor through delivery and the immediate postpartum period.
certBirth.adaBirthDoulaCertification: ADA's Birth Doula Certification will bring the same culturally integrated approach that defines our postpartum program: blending traditional Asian birthing practices with modern evidence-based care, delivered in the family's preferred language.
certBirth.howItDiffersFromPostpartumDoula: How It Differs from Postpartum Doula
certBirth.signUpForUpdates: Sign up to receive updates when Birth Doula enrollment opens.
certBirth.focusComfortAdvocacy: Focus: comfort, advocacy, emotional support during birth
certBirth.focusRecovery: Focus: recovery, newborn care, family adjustment

certIbclc.heroSubtitle: Multilingual preparation for the International Board Certified Lactation Consultant exam.
certIbclc.ibclcDescription: The International Board Certified Lactation Consultant (IBCLC) is the gold standard credential for lactation care professionals worldwide. Issued by the International Board of Lactation Consultant Examiners (IBLCE), it requires specialized education, clinical experience, and passing a rigorous exam.
certIbclc.ibclcCourseDescription: ADA's IBCLC Exam Prep course provides the lactation-specific education hours required by IBLCE — taught in multiple languages with a focus on breastfeeding practices and challenges unique to Asian families.
certIbclc.examRequirementsDescription: To sit for the IBCLC exam, candidates must complete: (1) health science education prerequisites, (2) lactation-specific education hours (which ADA's course fulfills), and (3) supervised clinical practice hours. Visit iblce.org for complete eligibility requirements.
certIbclc.getNotifiedDescription: Sign up to receive updates when the IBCLC Exam Prep course launches.
certIbclc.multilingualInstructionDescription: Study in English, Chinese, Japanese, or Korean. No other IBCLC prep program offers this breadth of language support.
certIbclc.culturallyIntegratedContentDescription: Our curriculum addresses breastfeeding beliefs, practices, and challenges specific to Asian cultures — from traditional confinement diet impacts on milk supply to family dynamics around breastfeeding decisions.
certIbclc.examFocusedCurriculumDescription: Covers all IBLCE content areas including anatomy, physiology, pharmacology, pathology, and clinical skills — structured specifically to prepare you for the IBCLC exam.
certIbclc.flexibleLearningDescription: Designed for working doulas and healthcare professionals. Combines self-paced study with live instruction sessions.
certIbclc.doesAdaIssueIbclcCredential: Does ADA issue the IBCLC credential?
certIbclc.doesAdaIssueIbclcCredentialAnswer: No. The IBCLC credential is issued by IBLCE (International Board of Lactation Consultant Examiners). ADA provides the exam preparation course that covers the lactation-specific education hours required to sit for the IBCLC exam.
certIbclc.doINeedToBeAdaCertifiedDoula: Do I need to be an ADA-certified doula first?
certIbclc.doINeedToBeAdaCertifiedDoulaAnswer: No. The IBCLC Exam Prep course is open to anyone who meets IBLCE’s eligibility requirements, including nurses, midwives, and other healthcare professionals.
certIbclc.whenWillTheCourseLaunchAnswer: We’re currently developing the curriculum. Sign up for notifications to be the first to know when enrollment opens.
certIbclc.howMuchWillItCostAnswer: Course pricing has not been finalized. We’ll share details when enrollment opens.

forFamilies.heroDescription: Where modern postpartum care meets thousand-year Asian traditions — because no new parent should recover alone.
forFamilies.fourthTrimesterText1: You come home from the hospital. The car seat feels impossibly heavy. Your body aches in ways no one warned you about. The baby is crying, and you realize that all the preparation in the world — the nursery, the registry, the birth plan — didn’t prepare you for this moment: the quiet overwhelm of being home, being responsible, being utterly exhausted.
forFamilies.fourthTrimesterText2: Your partner does their best. Your mother calls. Friends text congratulations. But between the midnight feedings and the uncertainty, you wonder: is this just how it is? Is this something I’m supposed to figure out alone?
forFamilies.fourthTrimesterText3: In Asian cultures, the answer has always been no.
forFamilies.fourthTrimesterText4: For thousands of years, Asian families have recognized that the weeks after birth are not just about the baby — they are about the mother. A dedicated period of rest, nourishment, and supported recovery. This is the tradition that ADA brings into your home.
forFamilies.traditionTitle: A Tradition Older Than Modern Medicine
forFamilies.traditionDescription: Across Asia, postpartum recovery isn’t a suggestion — it’s a practice woven into the fabric of family life.
forFamilies.recoveryStats: In the United States, 1 in 7 mothers experience postpartum depression. Studies show that continuous doula support significantly reduces this risk. The science is confirming what Asian families have known for millennia: dedicated postpartum care isn’t a luxury — it is a necessity.
forFamilies.doulaDescription: Forget the vague promises of “culturally competent care.” Here is what an ADA-certified doula brings into your home, day by day.
forFamilies.notJustForAsianFamiliesText1: “Do I need to be Asian to benefit from an ADA-certified doula?”
forFamilies.notJustForAsianFamiliesText2: No. The principles behind dedicated postpartum recovery — rest, nourishment, emotional support, expert newborn care — are universal. ADA-certified doulas serve families of all backgrounds, adapting care to your specific needs and household.
forFamilies.notJustForAsianFamiliesText3: The tradition is the root. The care is for everyone.
forFamilies.insuranceCoverageDescription: ADA-certified doula services are recognized by major insurance providers. FSA/HSA eligible.
forFamilies.coverageInstructionsStep1: Call member services on the back of your insurance card.
forFamilies.coverageInstructionsStep2: Ask if your plan covers postpartum doula services.
forFamilies.coverageInstructionsStep3: Mention certification by the Asian Doula Alliance (ADA).
forFamilies.coverageInstructionsStep4: Ask about pre-authorization requirements or visit limits.

findDoula.heroDescription: ADA certifies doulas to the highest standards of culturally integrated postpartum care. We can help connect you with a certified professional who speaks your language.
findDoula.trustedPartnerDescription: ADA works with select organizations whose doula teams are fully ADA-certified. Our primary partner serves families across the country with culturally integrated postpartum care.
findDoula.partnerDescription: Cooings specializes in culturally integrated postpartum doula care. Their entire roster consists of ADA-certified doulas serving families in Mandarin, Cantonese, Japanese, Korean, and English.
findDoula.insuranceSupport: Insurance support — Kaiser, Medi-Cal, Cigna, FSA/HSA
findDoula.serviceAreas: LA, Bay Area, Seattle, NYC, Chicago
findDoula.personalizedMatching: Personalized matching based on your needs
findDoula.recommendationDescription: Not sure where to start? We maintain relationships with certified doulas and partner agencies across the country. Tell us your location, language preference, and care needs — we'll personally recommend someone.

steps.title: Tell us your needs
steps.desc: Due date, location, language, cultural preferences.

steps.title: We recommend
steps.desc: We match you with certified doulas or partner agencies in your area.

steps.title: You choose
steps.desc: Meet your recommended doula and decide if it's the right fit.

findDoula.alreadyHaveDoulaDescription: If you've already found a doula through another channel, you can verify their ADA certification status using our public verification tool. Search by name, ID code, or certificate number.

certificationDetails.title: Rigorous Training
certificationDetails.desc: 7-module curriculum covering postpartum care, newborn care, lactation support, emergency response, and cultural competency.

certificationDetails.title: Comprehensive Examination
certificationDetails.desc: Certified doulas pass a multi-domain exam testing knowledge, practical skills, and ethical reasoning.

certificationDetails.title: Cultural Competency
certificationDetails.desc: Specialized training in Asian postpartum traditions — 坐月子, 산후조리, 里帰り — alongside evidence-based practices.

certificationDetails.title: Ongoing Standards
certificationDetails.desc: Annual renewal ensures doulas maintain current knowledge and adhere to our Code of Conduct.

findDoula.growingNetwork: Our network is growing. Contact us if your area is not listed.

howWeTrain.heroDescription: Every ADA-certified doula completes rigorous training that blends evidence-based care with cultural wisdom — before she ever enters your home.
howWeTrain.openingNarrative: Your doula didn’t just show up. She completed days of intensive training, passed both a written and practical exam, and earned a credential recognized by 6 insurance partners. She studied your culture’s postpartum traditions alongside evidence-based newborn care. She practiced emergency protocols until they became second nature.
howWeTrain.trainingCurriculumDescription: 7 modules. Every one designed so your doula is ready for whatever your postpartum journey brings.
howWeTrain.certificationProcessDescription: Three steps stand between your doula and her ADA credential.
howWeTrain.continuingEducationDescription1: Certification is not a one-time event. ADA doulas must maintain their credential through ongoing learning — attending workshops, completing updated coursework, and staying current with the latest in postpartum care and safety protocols.
howWeTrain.continuingEducationDescription2: When your doula arrives, she is not working from knowledge that is years old. She is current.
howWeTrain.insuranceRecognitionDescription: ADA certification is recognized by major insurance partners, which means your doula services may be covered.
howWeTrain.ctaDescription: Every ADA-certified doula has earned the training described above. Find one who speaks your language and understands your traditions.

renewCert.ctaDescription: Check your certification status or contact us to begin the renewal process.
renewCert.insurancePartnersList: Medi-Cal, Kaiser, Cigna, IEHP, Carrot Fertility, Progyny

codeOfConduct.heroDescription: Professional and ethical standards for ADA-certified doulas.
codeOfConduct.introText: As an ADA-certified doula, you are expected to uphold the highest standards of professional and ethical conduct. This Code of Conduct outlines the principles that guide our certified doulas in providing exceptional, culturally sensitive care.
codeOfConduct.enforcementDescription: Violations of this Code of Conduct may result in disciplinary action, including suspension or revocation of ADA certification. If you become aware of a potential violation, please report it to the Asian Doula Alliance at contact@asiandoula.org. All reports will be reviewed confidentially.

support.supportHeroDescription: Find answers, get in touch, or explore our resources.
support.faqCardDescription: Find answers to common questions about certification, training, exams, and doula services.
support.contactCardDescription: Reach out to our team. We respond within 1-2 business days.
support.articlesCardDescription: Read the latest news, guides, and stories from the ADA community.

supportFaq.heroDescription: Everything you need to know about ADA certification, training, and services.
supportFaq.ctaDescription: Our team responds within 1-2 business days.

supportContact.contactPageDescription: We'd love to hear from you. We respond within 1-2 business days.

programs.heroDescription: Certification tracks, training programs, and scholarships for aspiring doulas and lactation professionals.
programs.certificationTracksDescription: Choose the path that fits your career goals.
programs.scholarshipProgramDescription: Financial assistance for qualifying doula trainees. ADA believes cost should never be a barrier to certification.
programs.findATrainingDescription: Browse ADA-approved training programs in your area and language.

scholarship.heroDescription: Making doula certification accessible to aspiring caregivers from all backgrounds.
scholarship.removingBarriersDescription: ADA believes financial barriers should not prevent passionate individuals from becoming certified doulas. Our scholarship program exists to ensure that talent and dedication — not financial circumstances — determine who can pursue this meaningful career.
scholarship.scholarshipCoverage: Scholarships cover partial or full training and exam fees, removing the most significant obstacle for many aspiring doulas.
scholarship.priorityCandidates: Priority is given to candidates from underserved communities, multilingual speakers, and first-generation caregivers who bring unique perspectives and language skills to the families they serve.
scholarship.howToApplyDescription: Contact ADA at contact@asiandoula.org with the following:
scholarship.applicationReview: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.
scholarship.eligibilityItem1: Enrolled in or accepted to an ADA-approved training program
scholarship.eligibilityItem3: Commitment to serving Asian communities post-certification
scholarship.eligibilityItem4: Multilingual ability preferred but not required
scholarship.applicationItem2: Training program you are enrolled in or applying to

articles.headerDescription: Stay informed with the latest news, educational resources, and community stories from the Asian Doula Alliance.
articles.noArticlesFound: No articles found in this category.

verify.verifyCertificationStatus: Verify the certification status of any ADA-credentialed doula. This is an official verification service.
verify.searchPlaceholder: Name, Doula ID, or Certificate Number
verify.searchBy: Search by name, Doula ID, or certificate number
verify.noMatchingRecords: No matching records found for “{query}”. Please check the spelling or try a different search term.
verify.doulaFound: {count} doula{name}s found — select to view details:
verify.whyVerifyDescription: Confirms the doula has completed ADA's accredited training program and passed both written and practical certification exams under supervised conditions.
verify.whatYoullSeeDescription: Full credential details including certification type, current status, date issued, and validity period. All information is verified in real-time against our registry.
verify.openToEveryoneDescription: This service is available to expectant parents, insurance providers, healthcare facilities, and doula agencies. No registration required.
verify.searchStepDescription: Enter the doula's name, Doula ID (e.g. #25-311), or certificate number in the search field above.
verify.selectStepDescription: If multiple results are found, select the correct doula from the list to view their credential details.
verify.reviewStepDescription: View the official verification record showing the doula's credential type, certification status, and validity dates.
verify.adaCertificationStandardsDescription: Our doulas complete a comprehensive program including classroom training, supervised practice, and dual examinations before earning their credential.
verify.accessYourCredentialPortal: Access your credential portal to view records, download certificates, and check exam history.
